display_name: Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America
organization: Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA)
comment_letter:
RE: Formal Comments on FDA Proposed Rulemaking

Dear Commissioner,

Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA) appreciates the opportunity to provide these formal comments. As an entity engaged in brand-name pharmaceutical industry advocacy, drug pricing and market access policy, clinical trial and regulatory pathway coordination, our perspective is deeply informed by our operational environment, which includes R&D investment recovery timelines (10-15 years per drug), global regulatory harmonization needs, patent and exclusivity period dependencies.

We write today to have concerns regarding the proposed regulatory framework. Our primary feedback is as follows: Draft a comment expressing concern about regulatory changes that could delay drug approvals, requesting streamlined review pathways, and emphasizing innovation incentives.

The proposed changes will directly impact our operations in the following areas: compliance_cost, innovation_impact, implementation_timeline. We urge the Agency to consider these factors, particularly how they affect compliance cost, innovation impact, implementation timeline, to ensure that the final rule achieves its public health objectives without unintended consequences for the regulated community and the patients we serve.

We look forward to continued engagement with the FDA on this matter.

Sincerely,

Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America
Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA)

RE: Formal Comments on FDA Proposed Rulemaking

Dear Commissioner,

University of California San Francisco appreciates the opportunity to provide these formal comments. As an entity engaged in cancer drug approval standards research, accelerated approval outcomes analysis, surrogate endpoint validation studies, our perspective is deeply informed by our operational environment, which includes academic independence in FDA criticism, pharmaceutical industry research funding transparency, peer-review standards for regulatory science.

We write today to have concerns regarding the proposed regulatory framework. Our primary feedback is as follows: Draft a comment questioning the use of surrogate endpoints for accelerated approval, requesting mandatory confirmatory trial completion timelines, and supporting higher evidentiary standards.

The proposed changes will directly impact our operations in the following areas: patient_safety, scientific_integrity, innovation_impact. We urge the Agency to consider these factors, particularly how they affect patient safety, scientific integrity, innovation impact, to ensure that the final rule achieves its public health objectives without unintended consequences for the regulated community and the patients we serve.

We look forward to continued engagement with the FDA on this matter.

Sincerely,

Dr. Vinay Prasad, Oncology and Health Policy Researcher
University of California San Francisco
